steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Standard deviation is smallest when values cluster most tightly around the mean. Compare the range and spread of each dataset.

steps.title: Compare the spreads
steps.content: All four data sets are centered near $47$, so compare how far the values are from $47$. Choice D has values $46,47,47,47,48$, which are only $1$, $0$, $0$, $0$, and $1$ away from $47$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content:
• A ranges from $44$ to $50$, so it is more spread out.
  • B ranges from $45$ to $49$, so it is still more spread out than D.
  • C ranges evenly from $45$ to $49$.
  • D stays between $46$ and $48$, with three values exactly at $47$.
Choice D is the most tightly clustered, so it has the smallest standard deviation. D is correct.
